topic: Someone discovers their teenager has a secret social media account
raw_output:
[story 1]

The silence in the house had become a heavy, suffocating blanket. Liam traced the condensation on his glass of water, the ice cubes clinking with an irritating sharpness. He watched his daughter, Maya, retreating into her room, the door clicking shut with an almost audible finality. Lately, she’d been distant, her laughter sounding brittle, like thin glass about to shatter. It was this pervasive quiet that settled deep in his chest, a dull ache he couldn't quite name. While tidying up Maya’s discarded backpack, something caught his eye—a hidden icon on her phone's wallpaper, a faint, almost invisible watermark that didn't belong. A cold knot tightened in Liam’s stomach. He hesitated, the urge to investigate warring with a sudden, paralyzing fear of intrusion.

[story 2]

I spent the afternoon staring out the window at the rain-streaked glass, the grey light mirroring the landscape of my own mood. Everything felt muted, drained of color. Maya hadn't spoken to me properly all week; her responses were clipped, evasive. I was just trying to be helpful, clearing out her old tablet, when I stumbled upon the login details. It was a cascade of digital secrets, a hidden world she kept locked away. A strange, hollow feeling washed over me, a sense of profound disconnection. It wasn't anger that surfaced, just a deep, weary sadness, the kind that sinks into your bones and makes the air feel thin. I sat down heavily on the edge of the sofa, the weight of unspoken things pressing down on my shoulders.

[story 3]

The evening stretched ahead, slow and viscous. I found myself pacing the hallway, my footsteps echoing too loudly in the empty space. Maya was engrossed in her headphones, a small island of focused activity in the sea of her withdrawal. It was while retrieving a forgotten charger that I saw the notification pop up on her screen—a profile picture I didn't recognize, a stream of carefully curated images. My breath hitched. There was a profound sense of isolation settling over me, as if I were watching a life unfolding in a room I wasn't allowed to enter. I didn't approach her immediately. Instead, I just stood there, the quiet recognition of a secret life blooming inside me like a cold, unwelcome flower.
